name: Italian Pizzelle Using Anise
recipeIngredient: 2 1/4 cup melted shortening (use a combination of butter, margarine, and Crisco)
1 bottle anise extract (not as strong flavor as the oil)
recipeInstructions:
You will need a pizzelle iron to make these cookies. They can be found at Safeway, any good deli, specialty food store, or cooking store for about $50.
1. Whip the eggs until very frothy.
2. Add the sugar and cream well.
3. Add the melted shortening (make sure it is not hot) and cream well.
4. Add the flavorings and mix well.
5. Measure half the flour into a large bowl (8 quart size or better). Add the egg mixture and mix together with a heavy wooden spoon or any large spoon.
6. Gradually add the remaining half of the flour, one cup at a time, mixing well after each addition. The dough should be thick enough to "glom" off the spoon but still slightly runny.
7. Preheat the pizzelle iron for a few minutes.
8. Place 1 full teaspoon of dough in the center of each form and press closed tightly.
9. Cook for about 1 minute, then immediately move the cookie to wax paper to cool.
10. The cookies are soft at this point; make sure they are smooth on the wax paper.
11. Stack the cooled cookies and leave them in an open container. Do not cover them, as they will become soft and unappetizing. If possible, do not eat them for about 3 days to a week, as the anise flavor improves with age.
12. Enjoy.
